title: Novel process for the preparation of gadolinium complex of (4s)-4-(4-ethoxybenzyl)-3,6,9-tris(carboxylatomethyl)-3,6,9- triazaundecanedioic acid disodium (gadoxetate disodium)
abstract: The present invention discloses a novel process for the preparation of gadolinium complex of (4S)-4-(4-Ethoxybenzyl)-3,6,9-tris(carboxylatomethyl)-3,6,9- triazaundecanedioic acid disodium of formula 1 and novel intermediates thereof.
claims: Claims We claim: 1. A process for the preparation of gadolinium complex of (4S)-4-(4-Ethoxybenzyl)- 3,6,9-tris(carboxylatomethyl)-3,6,9-triazaundecanedioic acid disodium (Gadoxetate disodium) of formula 1 comprising the steps of: i) protecting amino group of L-tyrosine of formula 12 with di-tert-butyl dicarbonate (Boc 2 0) in presence of base to get boc-protected L-tyrosine of formula 13; alkylating Boc-protected L-tyrosine of formula 13 with diethyl sulfate in a suitable solvent to obtain formula 14; iii) converting formula 14 in to anhydride or ester in situ by reacting with suitable reagent followed by reduction with reducing agent to obtain (S)- tert-butyl (l-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropan -2-yl) carbamate of formula 15; iv) protecting hydroxyl group in (S)-tert-butyl (l-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-3- hydroxypropan -2-yl) carbamate with methanesulfonyl chloride in presence of base to obtain (S)-2-((tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino)-3-(4- ethoxyphenyl) propyl methanesulfonate of formula 16; condensing (S)-2-((tert-butoxy carbonyl) amino)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl) propylmethane sulfonate of formula 16 with ethylenediamine in a suitable solvent to obtain (S)-tert-butyl (l-((2-aminoethyl) amino)-3-(4- ethox henyl)propan-2-yl)carbamate of formula 17; deprotecting amino protecting from (S)-tert-butyl (l-((2-aminoethyl) amino)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl) propan-2-yl) carbamate using suitable acid to obtain (S)-Nl-(2-aminoethyl)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl)propane-l,2-diamine acid salt of formula 18, which is further converted in to free base of formula 18a in situ optionally, by reaction with a suitable base; alkylating (S)-Nl-(2-aminoethyl)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl) propane-1,2- diamine acid salt or free base with tertiary butyl bromoacetate in presence of potassium carbonate to provide (S)- tert- butyl 2,2'-(2- ((2-(bis(2 -tert- butoxy- 2-oxoethyl) amino)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl)propyl) (2-tert-butoxy-2- oxoethyl)amino) ethyl azanediyl) diacetate of formula 3; deprotecting tertiary butyl group of (S)- tert- butyl 2,2'-(2- ((2-(bis(2 -tert- butoxy- 2-oxoethyl) amino)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl)propyl) (2-tert-butoxy-2- oxoethyl)amino) ethyl azanediyl) diacetate of formula 3 with aqueous sodium hydroxide to obtain (S)-2,2'-((2-((2-(bis(carboxymethyl)amino)-3- (4-ethoxyphenyl) propyl) (carboxymethyl) amino)ethyl)azanediyl)diacetic acid of formula 4; converting (S)-2,2'-((2-((2-(bis(carboxymethyl)amino)-3-(4- ethoxyphenyl) propyl) (carboxymethyl) amino)ethyl)azanediyl)diacetic acid of formula 4 to Gadolinium salt by treating with Gadolinium(III) oxide in presence of sodium hydroxide. 2. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein the reducing agent of step-iii is selected from the group comprising of sodium cyanoborohydride, sodium triacetoxy boron hydride, lithium aluminium hydride and sodium borohydride. 3. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein hydroxyl protecting group of step-iv is selected from the group comprising of mesylates, tosylates, acetates and triflates. 4. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein the amino deprotecting reaction of step-vi is carried out in presence of p-toluene sulphonic acid or hydrochloric acid. 5. A process for preparation of Gadoxetate disodium of formula 1 comprising the steps of: a) alkylating compound of formula 18b or 18c or 18a using halo acetic acid in presence of sodium hydroxide to obtain intermediate of formula 4; b) treating (S)-2,2'-((2-((2-(bis(carboxymethyl)amino)-3-(4- ethoxyphenyl) propyl) (carboxymethyl) amino) ethyl) azanediyfjdiacetic acid of formula 4 with Gadolinium(III) oxide in presence water and sodium hydroxide to obtain Gadoxetate disodium of formula 1. The process as claimed in claim 5, wherein the halo acetic acid used is selected from the group comprising of bromoacetic acid, chloro acetic acid and iodoacetic acid. A process for preparation of Gadoxetate disodium of formula 1 with greater than 99% purity comprising the steps of: a) suspending compound of formula 4 in water and adjusting pH of the solution to 3.0 to 3.2 using acid resin; b) removing the resin and adding gadolinium oxide to the reaction mixture; c) heating the reaction mixture for 4-5 hrs. at 85-90Â°C; d) filtering and adjusting pH to 6.5 to 7.0 using base; e) treating with activated carbon at 40-45Â°C and filtering through hyflo bed. 8. The process as claimed in claim 7, wherein the acid resin used is selected from INDION 225H and 525H. 9. The process as claimed in claim 7, wherein the base used is selected from the group comprising of potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate andsodium carbonate. 10. A compound (S)-tert-butyl l-(4-ethoxyphenyl)-3-hydroxypropan-2-ylcarbamate of formula 15. 1. A compound (S)-2-((tert-butoxycarbonyl)amino)-3-(4-ethoxyphenyl) propyl methanesulfonate of formula 16. . A process for the preparation of (S)-2,2'-(2-((2-(bis(carboxymethyl)amino)-3 ethoxyphenyl)propyl)(carboxymethyl)amino)ethylazanediyl)diacetic acid formula 4 with greater than 99% purity comprising the steps of: i. suspending compound 3 in methanol and a ueous sodium hydroxide; ii. refluxing the solution for 5-6 hrs; iii. cooling to room temperature and adjusting pH to 6.5 to 7.0 by acidic resin; iv. filtering and washing the solid with a mixture of methanol and isopropyl alcohol.
